task: I’m drafting a short update for a supervisor about a project I handled, and I want it to be clear, professional, and easy to review. Can you help me phrase it so it sticks to the facts and the outcome without getting wordy?

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — keep it factual, outcome-focused, and easy to scan.

A good format is:

**Project / task:**  
**What I handled:**  
**Result / outcome:**  
**Any issue or follow-up:**  

Example:

**Project:** [Project name]  
**What I handled:** Completed [specific task] and verified [key check].  
**Result:** Work was finished on time and met requirements.  
**Follow-up:** [If needed, note any pending item or next step.]

If you want, paste your rough draft and I’ll tighten it into a clean supervisor update.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, that’s the right shape, but it’s a little generic. I’d want it tighter and more specific to the facts, no filler.

Something like:

Project: [name]
What I handled: Completed [task] and verified [check/measure].
Result: The work met requirements and was ready for the next step.
Follow-up: [only if there was an issue or handoff].

If you want, I can turn your rough draft into a clean supervisor note in that style.

turns.agent_response:
Yes — that’s the right style.

If you want it even tighter for a supervisor, I’d keep it to this:

**Project:** [name]  
**What I handled:** Completed [task] and verified [check/measure].  
**Result:** Met requirements and was ready for the next step.  
**Follow-up:** [Only if needed]

If you paste your rough draft, I can turn it into a clean version in that format.

user_simulator.text:
Closer. Still a little too template-ish.

I’d want it to sound like it came from an actual work note, not a form:

Project: [name]
What I handled: Completed [task], checked [specific measure/record/condition], and confirmed it met spec.
Result: Passed and was ready for the next step.
Follow-up: [only if there was a problem or handoff]

If you want, make it even tighter — like one sentence per line, no extra words.

turns.agent_response:
Yes — here’s the tighter version in that same work-note style:

**Project:** [name]  
**What I handled:** Completed [task], checked [specific measure/record/condition], and confirmed it met spec.  
**Result:** Passed and was ready for the next step.  
**Follow-up:** [only if there was a problem or handoff]

If you want, I can also make it sound a little more formal or more like a quick floor note.

user_simulator.text:
Closer, but still a little polished. I’d want it to sound more like a real note somebody wrote after the fact.

Keep the same three lines, just less dressed up:

Project: [name]
What I handled: Completed [task], checked [specific measure/record/condition], and it met spec.
Result: Passed and moved to the next step.
Follow-up: [only if needed]

If you can, make it even more plainspoken — like something I could send without it sounding edited up.

turns.agent_response:
Sure — keep it plain and factual:

**Project:** [name]  
**What I handled:** Completed [task], checked [specific measure/record/condition], and it met spec.  
**Result:** Passed and moved to the next step.  
**Follow-up:** [only if needed]

If you want, I can also turn your actual note into this style.

user_simulator.text:
Closer, but still a little cleaned up. I’d drop the bolding and keep it like a real note:

Project: [name]
What I handled: Completed [task], checked [specific measure/record/condition], and it met spec.
Result: Passed and moved to the next step.
Follow-up: [only if needed]

If you want, make it even plainer — more like something typed fast at the end of a shift.

turns.agent_response:
Yes — here’s the plainer version, with the same structure:

Project: [name]  
What I handled: Completed [task], checked [specific measure/record/condition], and it met spec.  
Result: Passed and moved to the next step.  
Follow-up: [only if needed]

If you want, I can make it even more like a quick end-of-shift note.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, closer, but still reads a little too cleaned up.

I’d want it more like:

Project: [name]
What I handled: Completed [task], checked [specific measure/record/condition], met spec.
Result: Passed and moved to the next step.
Follow-up: [only if needed]

That’s about the level I mean. If you want, make it even more like a quick note from the floor — less polished, more plain.

turns.agent_response:
Got it. Use this:

Project: [name]  
What I handled: Completed [task], checked [specific measure/record/condition], met spec.  
Result: Passed and moved to the next step.  
Follow-up: [only if needed]

If you want, paste the actual project details and I’ll turn it into that exact style.

user_simulator.text: Yeah. That’s the style I wanted.
